2605.18702.title: Distilling Tabular Foundation Models for Structured Health Data
2605.18702.paper_summary: Tabular foundation models (TFMs) achieve strong performance on health datasets, but their inference cost and infrastructure requirements limit practical use. We study whether their predictive behavior can be transferred to lightweight tabular models through knowledge distillation. Since in-context TFMs condition on the training set at inference time, naive distillation can introduce context leakage; we address this with stratified out-of-fold teacher labeling. Across $19$ healthcare datasets, $6$ TFM teachers, $4$ student families, and several multi-teacher ensembles, we find that distilled students retain at least $90\%$ of teacher AUC, outperforming teachers in some cases, while running at least $26\times$ faster on CPU and preserving calibration and fairness critical for health applications. Moreover, multi-teacher averaging does not consistently improve over the best single teacher. Leakage-aware distillation is thus a viable route for bringing TFM-quality predictions into inference-constrained health settings.
2605.18702.paper_summary_zh: 表格基礎模型（TFMs）在健康數據集上表現出色，但其推斷成本和基礎設施需求限制了實際應用。我們研究了它們的預測行為是否可以通過知識蒸餾轉移到輕量級表格模型。由於上下文TFMs在推斷時依賴於訓練集，天真的蒸餾可能會引入上下文洩漏；我們通過分層的外部教師標記來解決這個問題。在$19$個醫療數據集中，$6$個TFM教師，$4$個學生家族，以及幾個多教師集成中，我們發現蒸餾後的學生至少保留了$90\%$的教師AUC，在某些情況下超越教師，同時在CPU上運行速度至少快$26\times$，並保持對健康應用至關重要的校準和公平性。此外，多教師平均並不總是優於最佳單一教師。因此，意識到洩漏的蒸餾是一條可行的路徑，可以將TFM質量的預測引入受限推斷的健康環境中。
2605.18702.author: Aditya Tanna et.al.
2605.18702.authors: Aditya Tanna, Nassim Bouarour, Mohamed Bouadi, Vinay Kumar Sankarapu, Pratinav Seth
